page: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 504.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 7.88 GB of memory per core. This instance includes 2458 GB of local SSD storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the E64ds_v4 demonstrates strong memory performance, particularly in uncached large-block read-only and write bandwidth tests, registering 216.19 GB/sec and 96.58 GB/sec respectively. Multi-threaded operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a strong bzip3 decompression rate of 248.82 GB/sec and a strong PassMark database operations score of 22,572.06. CPU-bound tasks, such as Geekbench multi-core compiling and ray tracing, perform in the average tier. Large-scale LLM text generation with a 70B model represents a performance bottleneck, yielding a weak result of 2.74 tokens/sec, whereas prompt processing for smaller 135M models is strong at 2465.44 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64ds_v4 offers a high-density memory-to-vCPU ratio combined with substantial local storage, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without requiring dedicated GPU hardware. The instance is well-suited for large relational databases, in-memory caching systems, data analytics, and enterprise applications that demand high memory bandwidth and local temporary storage.
description: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv4 family, powered by a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 504.0 GB of RAM and 2458 GB of local storage, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance excels in memory-intensive tasks, demonstrating strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and PassMark database operations. While its CPU-bound benchmarks and small-model LLM prompt processing are solid, large-model text generation remains a relative bottleneck. Lacking dedicated GPUs, this server is qualitatively cost-effective for high-density memory workloads. It is highly suited for large databases, in-memory caching, and data-heavy enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring substantial local storage and strong memory bandwidth for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 504 GB of RAM
2458 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory bandwidth and database operation performance
Optimized for large databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
No bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
